setting.note: Gospodinov's Sofia is the Bulgarian heart of a novel about a Europe seduced by its own past. A clinic that rebuilds vanished decades room by room spreads from Switzerland until whole countries vote on which era to move back into. The map holds to the mausoleum-haunted centre of the capital, from the yellow-paved political square out along Vitosha Boulevard.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Georgi Gospodinov, first published in Bulgarian as Времеубежище in 2020. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Time Shelter, Georgi Gospodinov (2020). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The ochre of Sofia's famous yellow cobblestones, the paving of the city's political centre. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
